summary: Thorough and well-structured integration contract with 30 criteria spanning all major subsystems. Criteria are specific, testable, and cover the sprint theme comprehensively: loading, stage structure, progression/softlock prevention, save system integrity, game feel mechanics, performance, boss balance, world-specific mechanics, audio integration, state machine completeness, error-free operation, HUD, and collectibles. Regression coverage lists all 11 prior sprints. The mix of automation (17 criteria) and code-inspection (13 criteria) is appropriate for a final integration sprint.

criteriaAnalysis.assessment: PASS — Testable. Loads page, checks console for errors, verifies canvas and title text. Clear threshold.
PASS — Testable. Iterates all 13 stage IDs, calls loadStage, checks width/height > 0. Exhaustive.
PASS — Testable with minor weakness. Spawn/exit point validation is solid. The 'exitX > spawnX' reachability proxy is simplistic but pragmatic for an integration test.
issues: Reachability check (exitX > spawnX) is a weak proxy — doesn't prove path exists. Acceptable for integration-level validation.

criteriaAnalysis.assessment: PASS — Testable. Checks bossData, type string, spawn coords, and bossArenaX for all 13 stages.
PASS — Testable. Cross-references coin positions against WorldMap.STAGES totalCoins. Good data integrity check.
PASS — Testable. Validates enemy spawn entries have type/x/y for all stages.
PASS — Testable. Tests linear unlock chain from clean save through multiple stages.

PASS — Code inspection is pragmatic here. Automating a full boss fight would be extremely complex. The four inspection steps cover the critical flow: exit zone gating, boss arena walls, boss defeat enabling exit, and state transition.
issues: Runtime automation would be ideal but code inspection is a reasonable compromise for boss-defeat-to-exit flow.

criteriaAnalysis.assessment: PASS — Code inspection for victory state reachability. Checks state transition, stat calculation, and rendering.
PASS — Testable automation. Tests save round-trip for two stages with specific values.
PASS — Excellent test. Validates both directions: worse values don't overwrite, better values do. Three-step test pattern.
PASS — Testable. Verifies clearSave resets all save data including lives.
PASS — Pinned constant (COYOTE_FRAMES=6) with behavioral verification through code inspection.
PASS — Pinned constant (JUMP_BUFFER_FRAMES=8) with behavioral verification through code inspection.
PASS — Pinned constant (VARIABLE_JUMP_MULTIPLIER=0.4) with behavioral verification.
PASS — Pinned constant (WALL_SLIDE_SPEED=2) with state entry and wall jump verification.
PASS — Testable. Depends on window.__gameDebug API (documented in game.js). Threshold of 50 FPS is reasonable for title screen.
issues: Depends on __gameDebug existing; if missing, test fails. This is acceptable since game.js provides a debug API.

criteriaAnalysis.assessment: PASS — Testable. 45 FPS during gameplay is a reasonable threshold accounting for stage complexity.
PASS — Code inspection for particle lifecycle management. Checks lifetime, removal, and optional cap.
PASS — Excellent specificity. All 13 boss maxHealth values enumerated. The +-1 tolerance is slightly generous but acceptable for balance tuning.
issues: +-1 tolerance could mask off-by-one bugs. Consider exact match only.

criteriaAnalysis.assessment: PASS — Testable. Complements C-04 by testing actual spawn function rather than just data.
PASS — Pinned constants (ICE_FRICTION=0.97, ICE_ACCELERATION=0.04) with physics integration verification.
PASS — Code inspection for auto-scroll in stage 3-3. Checks mechanism and player penalty.
PASS — Testable. Enumerates all 11 required audio methods by name.
PASS — Testable. Lists all 8 game states and verifies update/render handlers for each.
PASS — Code inspection for pause/resume toggle. Verifies stage renders behind pause overlay.
PASS — Testable. Enumerates all 17 global objects by name.
PASS — Excellent end-to-end flow test. Covers title -> map -> stage -> pause -> resume -> map. Zero-error threshold is appropriately strict for a final sprint.
PASS — Code inspection for HUD rendering: hearts, coins, stage name, conditional boss HP bar.
PASS — Code inspection for collectible pickup flow: collision, removal, counter increment, sound.

sprintThemeElements.60fps optimization: Covered by C-17, C-18 (performance thresholds) and C-19 (particle bounds)
sprintThemeElements.game feel verification: Covered by C-13 (coyote time), C-14 (jump buffer), C-15 (variable jump), C-16 (wall slide)
sprintThemeElements.full playthrough validation: Covered by C-01 (load), C-02 (all stages), C-07 (progression), C-08 (no softlocks), C-09 (victory), C-28 (full flow)
sprintThemeElements.collectible accuracy: Covered by C-05 (coin placement), C-30 (collection mechanics)
sprintThemeElements.boss balance: Covered by C-04 (boss data), C-20 (health values), C-21 (spawning)
sprintThemeElements.no softlocks: Covered by C-03 (spawn/exit), C-07 (unlock chain), C-08 (boss-to-exit flow), C-09 (victory reachable)

gaps: No explicit criterion for death/respawn/lives system — covered by regression from sprints 2-3
No explicit criterion for iris-wipe transitions — covered by regression from sprint 11
No explicit criterion for controls screen functionality — covered by regression from sprint 3
coverageAnalysis.verdict: All sprint theme elements are covered. Identified gaps are appropriately delegated to regression testing.

regressionAnalysis.verdict: Complete — all 11 prior sprints included in regression testing.

outOfScopeAnalysis: Appropriate. Excludes new content creation, mechanic changes, audio redesign, visual effect changes, mobile/multiplayer, and resolution changes. Correctly scoped to integration/validation only.
notes: 30 criteria is high count but appropriate for the final integration sprint covering all subsystems.
Good balance of automation (17) vs code-inspection (13) — automation for data-driven checks, inspection for behavioral flow analysis.
C-20's +-1 health tolerance is slightly generous; evaluator should prefer exact match and only use tolerance if values are clearly intentional rounding.
C-28 is the most valuable criterion — an end-to-end flow test that exercises state transitions, rendering, input handling, and lifecycle management in one pass.
